description: Python-to-JavaScript module mapping for skill-translator. Used by the Dry-Run-Pass to classify imports as mappable, partial (with caveats), or unmappable. The translator emits a user-facing modal when partial or unmappable imports are present.

viaValues.npm: Use an npm package via the sandbox esbuild bundler (script imports it as ES module).
viaValues.built-in-tool: Operation should NOT run in the sandbox. The translator routes the call to a Vault Operator built-in tool (create_pptx, create_docx, etc.) instead of translating the Python code.
viaValues.partial: Partial mapping exists with documented limitations.
viaValues.unmappable: No reasonable JavaScript equivalent; user must either accept partial translation or fall back to skill-creator.

json.notes: Direct mapping. json.loads -> JSON.parse, json.dumps -> JSON.stringify.

os.notes: os.path.join -> path.join. File system operations route through ctx.vault sandbox bridge.

sys.notes: sys.argv -> use execute(args) signature. sys.stdout.write -> return value or console.debug.

re.notes: re.search -> str.match, re.sub -> str.replace. Watch for Python-specific regex features (named groups syntax differs).

datetime.notes: datetime.now() -> new Date(). Use ISO strings for serialization. For complex parsing prefer date-fns via npm.

pathlib.notes: Path('foo')/'bar' -> path.join('foo','bar'). All file ops route through ctx.vault.

io.notes: io.StringIO -> string concatenation. io.BytesIO -> Uint8Array. File reads via ctx.vault.read.

csv.notes: csv.reader -> Papa.parse. csv.writer -> Papa.unparse. Handles delimiters + quoting.

yaml.notes: yaml.safe_load -> yaml.load with FAILSAFE_SCHEMA. yaml.dump -> yaml.dump.

requests.notes: requests.get -> ctx.requestUrl({url, method:'GET'}). Replaces all HTTP via the sandbox bridge (CORS-safe).
limitations: No multipart upload (use ctx.vault.writeBinary then ctx.requestUrl)

urllib.notes: urllib.request.urlopen -> ctx.requestUrl. Same constraints as requests.

pdfplumber.notes: PDF text extraction should NOT happen in the sandbox. The translator emits a Body-instruction that uses read_document for input and the appropriate built-in tool for output.

PyPDF2.notes: Same as pdfplumber. Avoid translating binary PDF manipulation; reroute to built-in tools.

python-pptx.notes: PPTX generation routes through the built-in create_pptx tool (template-mode) or plan_presentation (content planning). DO NOT translate Presentation/Slide manipulation.

python-docx.notes: DOCX generation routes through the built-in create_docx tool. DO NOT translate Document/Paragraph manipulation.

openpyxl.notes: XLSX generation routes through the built-in create_xlsx tool. DO NOT translate Workbook/Worksheet manipulation.

pandas.notes: Common pandas patterns (DataFrame.head, .groupby, .to_csv) map to arrays + danfojs. Complex statistical operations may need manual coding.

numpy.notes: Element-wise math maps to Array.map. Matrix ops need ndarray or manual nested-loop translation. For scipy-grade math, fall back to skill-creator.

scipy.notes: No comparable scientific-computing library exists in JavaScript. The translator stops the conversion when scipy is imported.
limitations: Hard stop: signals, statistics, optimization, integration

matplotlib.notes: Plot generation should produce data + use generate_canvas or external charting; matplotlib code itself does not translate.

sklearn.notes: ML model inference may be possible via ONNX, but training pipelines do not translate.

subprocess.notes: Process spawning is not available in the sandbox by design (security).

shutil.notes: shutil.copyfile -> read + write through ctx.vault. shutil.rmtree -> ctx.vault.list + per-file remove (not exposed; needs plugin op).

tempfile.notes: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile -> write into the plugin tmp folder. Cleanup is automatic on task end.

hashlib.notes: hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest() -> crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', buf) + hex. Async API in browser.

collections.notes: Counter -> manual reduce + Map. defaultdict -> use Map with || init. OrderedDict -> Map preserves insertion order.

itertools.notes: chain -> spread/concat. groupby -> manual reduce. permutations -> needs manual algorithm or a small helper.
limitations: No lazy-evaluation generators by default; manual implementation needed

functools.notes: lru_cache -> manual Map memoization. partial -> arrow function bind.

typing.notes: Type hints are dropped in translation; TypeScript types may be added in a follow-up if the target is .ts.

logging.notes: logger.info -> console.debug. logger.warning -> console.warn. logger.error -> console.error. No file handler in sandbox.

argparse.notes: Argparse defines a CLI shape; in Vault Operator scripts the `execute(args)` parameter object replaces it. The translator inlines a destructuring pattern at the top of execute().

grep.notes: Read the target file via ctx.vault and apply a JS regex.

sed.notes: Simple substitutions map to string.replace. Multi-line sed scripts may need manual translation.

awk.notes: Simple field extraction maps to split + map. Complex awk programs (associative arrays, BEGIN/END blocks) need careful translation.
limitations: Multi-pattern programs may need manual rewriting

python.notes: Recursive python invocation is unmappable; the translator focuses on direct Python source files.

pip.notes: Package installation is not part of the runtime; the translator surfaces unmappable on encountering pip calls.
